result.markdown:
--- title: "Interactive World Map" --- # Interactive Prototype This page embeds the interactive visualization prototype directly into the documentation website. It is the same map as the standalone Flask app ([`src/visualize_master_data.py`](../src/visualize_master_data.py)), but rebuilt as a self-contained Plotly figure so it works as a **static, deployable HTML page** with no server. Use the dropdown in the top-left of the map to switch between the pixel-level aridity heatmap and the three country-level choropleth metrics. Hover over any country (or grid point) for details. ::: {#cell-fig-interactive-map .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code code-fold="true"} from pathlib import Path import pandas as pd import plotly.graph_objects as go # --- Locate output_data/ regardless of Quarto's execute directory ---------- ROOT = Path.cwd() while not (ROOT / "output_data").exists() and ROOT != ROOT.parent: ROOT = ROOT.parent MASTER_CSV = ROOT / "output_data" / "aggregated_master_data.csv" ARIDITY_GRID_CSV = ROOT / "output_data" / "aridity_grid.csv" df = pd.read_csv(MASTER_CSV) grid = pd.read_csv(ARIDITY_GRID_CSV) # Drop ocean / no-data points: real land aridity is always > 0, only # ocean and no-data pixels sit at exactly 0 and would flood the map. grid = grid[grid["aridity_index"] > 0] # --- Pastel palette (shared with the docs theme) --------------------------- PAPER = "#cacaca" INK = "#2f3142" INK_SOFT = "#6a6c7d" LINE = "#e0dcef" # Map base: clear separation between water and landmass OCEAN = "#bccadf" # distinct slate-blue so water is visible LAND = "#f4f1ea" # neutral warm land so pastel pixels stand out COAST = "#8f9cb3" # defined coastline for a crisp land/ocean edge ARIDITY_SCALE = [[0.0, "#f6e6c5"], [0.5, "#9bd0c0"], [1.0, "#7fa8d9"]] DENSITY_SCALE = [[0.0, "#fbede4"], [0.5, "#efb59c"], [1.0, "#df8fa6"]] GDP_SCALE = [[0.0, "#eef5ec"], [0.5, "#a9d8c2"], [1.0, "#7fc8b0"]] # --- Shared light geo layout ---------------------------------------------- GEO_LAYOUT = dict( showframe=False, showcoastlines=True, coastlinecolor=COAST, showland=True, landcolor=LAND, showocean=True, oceancolor=OCEAN, showcountries=True, countrycolor="#d8d2c4", showlakes=True, lakecolor=OCEAN, projection=dict(type="natural earth"), bgcolor=OCEAN, ) HOVERLABEL = dict( bgcolor="#ffffff", bordercolor="#8b80d6", font=dict(family="Inter, sans-serif", size=13, color=INK), ) # Country-level metrics: (column, label, colorscale, zmin, zmax) METRICS = [ ("aridity_index", "Aridity Index (avg)", ARIDITY_SCALE, 0, 2.5), ("population_density_2022", "Pop. Density (ppl/km²)", DENSITY_SCALE, 0, 500), ("gdp_per_capita_2024", "GDP per Capita (USD)", GDP_SCALE, 0, 100000), ] # Per-country hover text (matches buildHoverText in the Flask app) customdata = df[ [ "country_name", "country_code", "region", "income_group", "aridity_index", "population_density_2022", "gdp_per_capita_2024", ] ].values HOVERTEMPLATE = ( "%{customdata[0]}
" "─────────────────────
" "Code: %{customdata[1]}
" "Region: %{customdata[2]}
" "Income: %{customdata[3]}
" "─────────────────────
" "🌡️ Aridity: %{customdata[4]:.4f}
" "👥 Pop. Density: %{customdata[5]:,.2f} /km²
" "💰 GDP/cap: $%{customdata[6]:,.0f}" "" ) fig = go.Figure() # --- Trace 0: pixel-level aridity heatmap ---------------------------------- fig.add_trace( go.Scattergeo( lat=grid["lat"], lon=grid["lon"], mode="markers", marker=dict( size=4, opacity=0.7, color=grid["aridity_index"], colorscale=ARIDITY_SCALE, cmin=0, cmax=2.5, colorbar=dict( title=dict(text="Aridity Index", font=dict(color=INK_SOFT, size=12)), tickfont=dict(color=INK_SOFT), bgcolor="#ffffff", bordercolor=LINE, outlinecolor=LINE, len=0.6, ), line=dict(width=0), ), hovertemplate=( "Aridity Index: %{marker.color:.4f}" "
Lat: %{lat:.2f}° Lon: %{lon:.2f}°" ), hoverlabel=HOVERLABEL, name="Aridity (heatmap)", visible=True, ) ) # --- Traces 1-3: country-level choropleths --------------------------------- for col, label, scale, zmin, zmax in METRICS: fig.add_trace( go.Choropleth( locations=df["country_code"], z=df[col], zmin=zmin, zmax=zmax, customdata=customdata, hovertemplate=HOVERTEMPLATE, hoverlabel={**HOVERLABEL, "align": "left"}, colorscale=scale, colorbar=dict( title=dict(text=label, font=dict(color=INK_SOFT, size=12)), tickfont=dict(color=INK_SOFT), bgcolor="#ffffff", bordercolor=LINE, outlinecolor=LINE, len=0.6, ), marker_line=dict(color="#cfcadf", width=0.5), name=label, visible=False, ) ) # --- Metric switcher (replaces the HTML

<script type="application/javascript">define('jquery', [],function() {return window.jQuery;})</script>
<script>
window.PlotlyConfig = {MathJaxConfig: 'local'};
if (window.MathJax && window.MathJax.Hub && window.MathJax.Hub.Config) {window.MathJax.Hub.Config({SVG: {font: "STIX-Web"}});}
</script>
<script type="module">import "https://cdn.plot.ly/plotly-3.5.0.min"</script>
